description: You are a field robotics researcher who needs to build a complete SLAM (Simultaneous Localization and Mapping) data-collection benchmark from scratch in Webots. Create a world containing: a 10m x 8m RectangleArena floor; four internal corridor walls as Solid Box nodes (DEF WALL_N at (0, 0.5, -2.5) size 6x1x0.2, WALL_S at (0, 0.5, 2.5) size 6x1x0.2, WALL_E at (3.5, 0.5, 0) size 0.2x1x3, WALL_W at (-3.5, 0.5, 0) size 0.2x1x3) each with Physics and boundingObject; a Pioneer 3-AT robot (DEF SLAM_ROBOT) at (-4, 0.1, 0) equipped with a Lidar (name 'scanner', 1 layer, FOV 3.14159, horizontalResolution 360, maxRange 8.0), GPS (name 'gps'), InertialUnit (name 'imu'), and two PositionSensors (names 'left_encoder' and 'right_encoder', resolution 0.001) on the wheel joints; controller set to 'slam_logger'. WorldInfo: basicTimeStep 16, title 'SLAM Data Collection Benchmark'. Then write a Python controller at /home/ga/webots_projects/slam_bench/controllers/slam_logger/slam_logger.py that initializes the Lidar (with point cloud enabled), GPS, IMU, and both wheel encoders. In the main simulation loop, read all sensors each timestep and append a CSV line to /tmp/slam_log.csv with columns: timestamp, gps_x, gps_y, gps_z, imu_roll, imu_pitch, imu_yaw, left_enc, right_enc, lidar_range_count. Set both wheel motors to velocity 3.0 for forward driving. Save the world to /home/ga/Desktop/slam_benchmark.wbt.
